title: To amend the Small Business Act with respect to the maximum additional loan amount for certain disaster loans, and for other purposes.
plain_summary: This bill changes the rules for disaster loans that help small businesses after emergencies like hurricanes or floods. Right now, if a business gets a disaster loan, they can borrow up to 20 percent more money on top of their original loan amount. This bill would increase that limit from 20 percent to 30 percent. This means businesses could get more extra money to help them recover and rebuild after a disaster damages their property or hurts their business.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Small Business.

sections.title: Section 1  Maximum Additional Loan Amount for Certain Disaster Loans
sections.text: This section changes the Small Business Act. It updates the rules about how much extra money can be borrowed through certain disaster loans. The law currently allows an additional twenty percent of the loan amount. This bill changes that limit to thirty percent of the loan amount.
